attacking_style: Does not rely on pure climbing attacks; instead survives difficult terrain then capitalizes with sprint finish when terrain eases or on flatter conclusions.
team_tactics: Heavy reliance on teammates for pacing back after being distanced on climbs; team shields weaknesses rather than launches attacks, then positions for final sprint
terrain_preference: Stage profiles with climbs followed by recovery sections or sprint finishes; struggles on sustained gradients but excels when can use sprint after climbing
vulnerability: Sustained climbing exposes this rider - gets dropped on prolonged ascents and depends entirely on teammates to limit losses and facilitate recovery
key_patterns: Dropped on climbs but recovers with team pacing support (observed Tour de Pologne)
Strong finishing speed after surviving difficult terrain enables stage wins (Tour de Pologne victory)
Relies on domestique work to compensate climbing vulnerability, not self-sufficient on mountains
form_trajectory: positive: despite climbing struggles, showed ability to win both stage and overall with tactical intelligence and finishing power
